name: Mercer Ellington
abstract: Mercer Kennedy Ellington (March 11, 1919 – February 8, 1996) was an American musician, composer, and arranger. His father was Duke Ellington, whose band Mercer led for 20 years after his father's death.

concerts.label: WLIB Festival of Music and Drama

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: when I joined Mercer Ellington’s band... you didn’t know I played with Mercer, right?
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Erskine Hawkins
about_quotes.evidence: And then, I stayed, he's always been a close friend, you know.

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Whaley
about_quotes.evidence: Mercer Ellington, he used to buy me cigars... I can't understand why no one would call on me -- ... They know how I -- how close I was with Duke, and knew everything and even Mercer

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWEE] Who? ... [INTERVIEWER] Mercer Ellington's book; this book, 'Duke Ellington In Person', the one that Mercer wrote. [INTERVIEWEE] Mercer's book?

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: the first writing I did was with Mercer Ellington. Mercer had the band... I wrote some, Billy Strayhorn wrote some, Mercer wrote some.

about_quotes.speaker: Candido Camero
about_quotes.evidence: And after that, I also worked with his son, Mercer Ellington. I worked with his son also after Duke Ellington.
about_quotes.doc_title: Candido Camero — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
